summary: Notion AI is aggressively expanding its enterprise sales and customer success capabilities globally, with a concentrated hiring push across mid-market, commercial, and enterprise sales roles—including regional coverage in DACH, EMEA, Asia, and AMER. Concurrently, it is investing in infrastructure and security foundations for AI-powered offerings, evidenced by a new Pulumi SDK for Elasticstack and a dedicated AI Security engineering role. This signals a strategic pivot toward scalable, secure, and commercially mature AI platform delivery.

signals.title: New Pulumi SDK for Elasticstack Terraform provider
signals.insight: Indicates Notion AI is building robust, infrastructure-as-code–enabled observability and logging capabilities—critical for production-grade AI systems and enterprise compliance.

signals.title: Enterprise Account Executive, San Francisco
signals.insight: Signals prioritization of high-touch enterprise sales in the core US tech hub, suggesting imminent expansion of AI-powered enterprise contracts and integrations.

signals.title: Sales Manager, Asia
signals.insight: Reveals formalized geographic expansion into Asia—a key growth market—indicating localized go-to-market strategy for Notion AI beyond North America and EMEA.

signals.title: Application Security Engineer, AI Security
signals.insight: Highlights growing emphasis on AI-specific security posture—directly addressing enterprise buyers' top concern and differentiating Notion AI from less-secured AI competitors.

signals.title: Manager, Scaled Customer Success
signals.insight: Suggests Notion AI is shifting from reactive to proactive, scalable CS models—essential for retaining and expanding AI seat-based or usage-based revenue streams.

signals.title: Software Engineer, Datastore
signals.insight: Points to foundational investment in low-latency, AI-optimized data storage—critical for real-time RAG, personalization, and complex workspace intelligence features.

signals.title: Account Executive, Mid-Market, DACH
signals.insight: Confirms targeted expansion into German-speaking enterprise markets, where data sovereignty and AI governance requirements are stringent and competitive.

signals.title: Solutions Engineer, EMEA
signals.insight: Indicates growing technical sales capacity to support complex AI use-case deployments and integration narratives in regulated EMEA markets.
